title: Your Name is Mine

content.problemStatement:
In an attempt to control the rise in population, **Archer** was asked to come up with a plan. This time he is targeting marriages. Archer, being as intelligent as he is, came up with the following plan:

A man with name **M** is allowed to marry a woman with name **W**, only if **M** is a subsequence of **W** or **W** is a subsequence of **M**.

**A** is said to be a subsequence of **B**, if **A** can be obtained by deleting some elements of **B** without changing the order of the remaining elements.

Your task is to determine whether a couple is allowed to marry or not, according to Archer's rule.
constraints: All characters are lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two space-separated strings M and W, the names of the man and the woman.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print YES if either M is a subsequence of W or W is a subsequence of M, otherwise print NO.

sampleTestCases.input:
3
john johanna
ira ira
kayla jayla
sampleTestCases.explanation: Case 1: "john" is a subsequence of "johanna". Case 2: identical strings are subsequences of each other. Case 3: neither string is a subsequence of the other.
First pair: strings are equal, so YES. Second pair: "abc" cannot be obtained from "def" and vice‑versa, so NO.
"hello" is not a subsequence of "world" and "world" is not a subsequence of "hello".

hidden.label: stress case
hidden.input:
3
abc abcde
xyz xy
pqr pqr

hidden.label: stress case 2
hidden.input:
4
aaa aaaa
aab aab
abc abc
abcd abcde

hidden.label: stress case 4
hidden.input:
5
m n
op opq
rst uvw
xyz xyz
abc abc

solution.approach: For each pair of strings we need to check whether one is a subsequence of the other. This can be done with a two‑pointer scan: iterate over the longer string and advance a pointer in the shorter string whenever the current characters match. If the pointer reaches the end of the shorter string, it is a subsequence. Perform the check in both directions; if either succeeds, the answer is YES, otherwise NO. The scan is linear in the total length of the two strings.
solution.timeComplexity: O(|M| + |W|) per test case, O(total length of all strings) overall.
